revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24644718927|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:有間神社]] {{Expand Japanese|有間神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Arima Shrine | native_name = 有間神社 | image = Arimaphoto.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Arima Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.814182|135.21928}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|es|Ame-no-minaka-nushi-no-kami|fa|آمه-نو-میناکانوشی|fr|Ame-no-Minakanushi|id|Ame-no-Minakanushi|it|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|mni|ꯑꯃꯦ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯅꯀꯅꯨꯁꯤ|pl|Amenominakanushi|ru|Амэноминакануси|simple|Ame-no-Minakanushi|uz|Ame-no Minakanusi|zh|天之御中主神|qid=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Arima Shrine'''|有間神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160338|ltq=Q135160338|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11498550|ltq=Q11498550|qid=Q11498550|1=List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 57 ** P155: {{ill|Minume Shrine|lt=Minume Shrine|ja|敏馬神社|qid=Q11499217|1=Minume Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q11392062|ltq=Q11392062|qid=Q11392062|1=Kuchi Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1078814|ltq=Q1078814|qid=Q1078814|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}} * {{ill|Settsu Province|lt=Settsu Province|ca|Província de Settsu|cs|Provincie Seccu|de|Provinz Settsu|en|Settsu Province|eo|Provinco Seccu|es|Provincia de Settsu|fa|ولایت ستسو|fr|Province de Settsu|gl|Provincia de Settsu|hak|Settsu-koet|id|Provinsi Settsu|it|Settsu (provincia)|ja|摂津国|ko|셋쓰국|lt|Seccu žemė|nl|Settsu (provincie)|pt|Província de Settsu|ru|Сэтцу (провинция)|simple|Settsu Province|sk|Seccu (provincia)|sr|Провинција Сецу|tr|Settsu (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Сеццу|zh_min_nan|Settu-kok|zh_yue|攝津國|zh|攝津國|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} * {{ill|Q11516836|ltq=Q11516836|qid=Q11516836|1=Arima district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.814182|135.21928}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Arimaphoto.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q2842779|ltq=Q2842779|qid=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181077 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ありまじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2140005000599 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県神戸市北区有野町有野4435', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:有間神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11516720}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Arima Shrine |Image = [[File:Arimaphoto.jpg|270px|Arima Shrine (August 1, 2012)]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe City|Q48320|qid=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Kobe)|Q1078814|qid=Q1078814|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Arino-chō|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Arino|1=UNKNOWN}} 4435 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined Deities = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Ōkami|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Ōkami|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|Q2842779|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Ōkami|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}
{{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Ōkami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=Three-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate Names = Sannō-gū
Arima Sōsha
Arima-sha |Annual Festival = {{ill|lt=Health and Sports Day|UNKNOWN|lt=Second Monday of October}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Kobe|label=Arima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Arima Shrine''' (ありまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Arino-chō, Kita Ward, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. Its shrine crest is the “{{ill|lt=Tomoe|Q1557435|qid=Q1557435|lt=triple tomoe|1=Tomoe}}.” === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Ōkami|1=Ōkuninushi}}''' (おおなむちのおおかみ) * '''{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Ōkami|1=Sukunabikona}}''' (すくなひこなのおおかみ) * '''{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|Q2842779|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Ōkami|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}''' (あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ) – Enshrined in {{ill|lt=969|Q23708|qid=Q23708|1=969}} (1st year of {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|qid=Q1373003|1=Anna (era)}}). * '''{{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Ōkami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}''' (ことしろぬしのおおかみ) – Enshrined in {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|qid=Q18658|1=1935}} (10th year of {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}) from {{ill|lt=Miho Shrine|Q3313076|qid=Q3313076|1=Miho Shrine}}. === Name === The origin of the shrine’s name is unclear, but “Arima” is considered an older spelling of “Arima.” According to the history of Arino Village, “ari” means mountain, and “arima” refers to an open place among mountains. It is also unclear when the place-name “Arima” came to be written as such. Because the shrine was once located in Sannō Valley before its relocation, it is said to have formerly been called '''Sannō-gū'''. === History === In ancient times, the shrine was enshrined in Sannō Valley of Narae Village in Yamaguchi Manor (Arima District). The date of foundation is unknown, but according to shrine tradition, the earliest record is that {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q329761|qid=Q329761|1=Emperor Jomei}} visited and worshiped here in September {{ill|lt=631|Q31577|qid=Q31577|1=631}} (3rd year of Emperor Shōmu) when he traveled to {{ill|lt=Arima Onsen|Q4790673|qid=Q4790673|1=Arima Onsen}}. It is believed that the shrine existed even before that. In {{ill|lt=715|Q23528|qid=Q23528|1=715}} (1st year of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|qid=Q1373115|1=Reiki (era)}}), a massive flood from {{ill|lt=Mount Rokkō|Q462120|qid=Q462120|1=Mount Rokkō}} caused the {{ill|lt=Arima River (Hyōgo Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Arima River}} to overflow, washing away the shrine buildings. By divine oracle, the shrine was relocated to Nishio Village in {{ill|lt=Arino Village|Q18700240|qid=Q18700240|lt=Arino Manor|1=Arino}}. A temporary shrine was established until construction was completed; the place where the sacred object was temporarily enshrined later became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tao Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. Additionally, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okaba Wakamiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} was built at the site that served as the temporary resting place (otabisho) during the relocation. During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, when the {{ill|lt=Battle of Miki|Q11242477|qid=Q11242477|1=Siege of Miki}} occurred, the shrine buildings were damaged by warfare because the shrine priest, of the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|qid=Q1210114|1=Takeda clan}}, was related by marriage to the {{ill|lt=Bessho clan|Q2975222|qid=Q2975222|1=Bessho clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Miki Castle|Q11242473|qid=Q11242473|1=Miki Castle}}. In the Edo period, the shrine received donations from the lords of the {{ill|lt=Sanda Domain|Q10863363|qid=Q10863363|1=Sanda Domain}} such as {{ill|lt=Kuki Takakuni|Q11370504|qid=Q11370504|1=Kuki Takakuni}}, and later from the {{ill|lt=Okabe Domain|Q10863332|qid=Q10863332|1=Okabe Domain}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|lt=Bushū|1=Musashi Province}}, who became local rulers. Until the {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the adjacent “Sannōzan Jingū-ji” stood nearby, and the shrine was known as Sannō Gongen. The former temple site is now a parking lot, and the statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|qid=Q337624|1=Acala}} that stood within the precincts is enshrined as a subsidiary shrine. As the general guardian shrine of {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}’s {{ill|lt=Arima District|Q11516836|qid=Q11516836|1=Arima district}}, it attracted widespread devotion. Because the old name Sannō-gū is associated with “ubuno-o” (the umbilical cord), it was worshiped as a deity of safe childbirth. At the otabisho there is a “Koyasu Stone,” where pregnant women prayed for safe delivery, and after childbirth, mother and child were said to purify themselves in the river flowing before the shrine. It is also said that prayers were offered at the shrine for the healthy growth of newborns, and thus the shrine is revered as a guardian of children. The shrine grove (chinju no mori) covers approximately 28,000 square meters and has been selected as one of the {{ill|lt=Hyōgo’s 100 Selected Forests|Q11276623|qid=Q11276623|1=Hyōgo’s 100 Selected Forests}}. Of this, about 7,000 square meters of a grove of {{ill|lt=Castanopsis|Q1065400|qid=Q1065400|lt=shii|1=Castanopsis}} trees has been designated both as an environmental green conservation area by Hyōgo Prefecture and as a natural monument by Kobe City. === Chronology === * Date unknown – Founded in Narae Village of {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Village (Arima District, Hyōgo)|Q11466901|qid=Q11466901|lt=Yamaguchi Manor|1=Yamaguchi}}. * September {{ill|lt=631|Q31577|qid=Q31577|1=631}} (3rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q329761|qid=Q329761|1=Emperor Jomei}}) – Imperial visit. * October {{ill|lt=647|Q31111|qid=Q31111|1=647}} (3rd year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|qid=Q1077930|1=Taika (era)}}) – Visit by {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|qid=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}. * {{ill|lt=715|Q23528|qid=Q23528|1=715}} (1st year of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|qid=Q1373115|1=Reiki (era)}}) – Relocated to the current site by divine oracle. * {{ill|lt=851|Q29966|qid=Q29966|1=851}} (1st year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}}) – Granted Senior Sixth Rank, Upper. * April {{ill|lt=852|Q30383|qid=Q30383|1=852}} (Ninju 2) – Offerings presented by the imperial court. * December {{ill|lt=854|Q24981|qid=Q24981|1=854}} (1st year of {{ill|lt=Saikō|Q1324664|qid=Q1324664|1=Saikō}}) – Offerings presented by the imperial court. * February {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}} (1st year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}}) – Shrine buildings repaired; offerings and a fire-pacifying ritual conducted in April. * {{ill|lt=869|Q23917|qid=Q23917|1=869}} (Jōgan 11) – Prayers and offerings for the prevention of pirates, storms, floods, and earthquakes. * December {{ill|lt=871|Q24754|qid=Q24754|1=871}} (Jōgan 13) – Repairs and offerings due to a great epidemic. * November {{ill|lt=893|Q24762|qid=Q24762|1=893}} (5th year of {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|qid=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}}) – Granted First Rank. * {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}} (5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}}) – Listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. * {{ill|lt=969|Q23708|qid=Q23708|1=969}} (1st year of {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|qid=Q1373003|1=Anna (era)}}) – Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Ōkami enshrined. * {{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|qid=Q6712|1=1580}} (8th year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}}) – Shrine buildings burned during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Miki|Q11242477|qid=Q11242477|1=Siege of Miki}}. * {{ill|lt=1622|Q6815|qid=Q6815|1=1622}} (8th year of {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|qid=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}}) – Shrine buildings destroyed by fire. * {{ill|lt=1649|Q6945|qid=Q6945|1=1649}} (2nd year of {{ill|lt=Keian|Q1146135|qid=Q1146135|1=Keian}}) – {{ill|lt=Abe Nobumori|Q11451596|qid=Q11451596|1=Abe Nobumori}}, lord of the {{ill|lt=Okabe Domain|Q10863332|qid=Q10863332|1=Okabe Domain}} in {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|lt=Bushū|1=Musashi Province}}, becomes ruler of {{ill|lt=Arino Village|Q18700240|qid=Q18700240|lt=Arino Manor|1=Arino}}; thereafter the shrine serves as a place of prayer for the domain and receives three koku of brown rice annually. * {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|qid=Q6989|1=1736}} (1st year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}}) – {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|qid=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}} erects the shrine-name stone “Arima-sha” with permission from {{ill|lt=Temple and Shrine Magistrate|Q1036279|qid=Q1036279|1=Jisha-bugyō}} {{ill|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke|Q1054143|qid=Q1054143|1=Ōoka Tadasuke}} (donor: the 9th shogun {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieshige|Q323443|qid=Q323443|1=Tokugawa Ieshige}}). * {{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|qid=Q7772|1=1789}} (1st year of {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|qid=Q1195131|1=Kansei}}) – Shrine buildings destroyed by fire. * {{ill|lt=1796|Q7818|qid=Q7818|1=1796}} (Kansei 8) – Current main hall completed. * May {{ill|lt=1849|Q7650|qid=Q7650|1=1849}} (2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|qid=Q1146125|1=Kaei}}) – The imperial court bestows a fan in a willow box, vermilion lanterns with the imperial crest, and one kuroki torii. * {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|qid=Q7720|1=1869}} (2nd year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}) – The shrine temple is abolished due to the {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. * November {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) – Designated a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. * {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|qid=Q18658|1=1935}} (10th year of {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}) – Kotoshironushi-no-Ōkami enshrined from {{ill|lt=Miho Shrine|Q3313076|qid=Q3313076|1=Miho Shrine}}. * {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|qid=Q2091|1=1999}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 11) {{ill|lt=February 24|Q2355|qid=Q2355|1=February 24}} – About 7,000 square meters of the shrine grove designated a natural monument of Kobe City. * {{ill|lt=2011|Q1994|qid=Q1994|1=2011}} (Heisei 23) {{ill|lt=June 7|Q2616|qid=Q2616|1=June 7}} – The shrine office is completely destroyed by fire. * {{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|qid=Q1990|1=2012}} (Heisei 24) {{ill|lt=June 24|Q2665|qid=Q2665|1=June 24}} – The shrine office is rebuilt. * {{ill|lt=2015|Q2002|qid=Q2002|1=2015}} (Heisei 27) – Marks the 1,300th anniversary of the relocation. === Shrine buildings === === Main hall and worship hall === The current {{ill|lt=main hall (Shinto architecture)|Q3080012|qid=Q3080012|lt=honden|1=Shinto architecture}} was rebuilt in 1796 and is of the {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=three-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style. Formerly only the main hall and {{ill|lt=offering hall (Shinto architecture)|Q3080012|qid=Q3080012|lt=heiden|1=Shinto architecture}} existed, but after World War II a reinforced-concrete {{ill|lt=worship hall (Shinto architecture)|Q3080012|qid=Q3080012|lt=haiden|1=Shinto architecture}} was newly constructed. The kuroki torii standing before the worship hall was originally used for {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōmei|Q299179|qid=Q299179|1=Emperor Kōmei}}’s {{ill|lt=Daijōsai|Q11433653|qid=Q11433653|1=Daijosai}} and was bestowed in {{ill|lt=1849|Q7650|qid=Q7650|1=1849}}; after it decayed, it was rebuilt in concrete. === Subsidiary shrines within the precincts === * Kubomori Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine ({{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}) – Enshrined in 1900 * Tenjin Shrine ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) * Hachiman Shrine ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|lt=Inari Ōkami|Q719665|qid=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyōjin|1=Inari Ōkami}} (Tenkō Iwakō Daimyōjin; Tenjō Seishun Daimyōjin)) * Bushin Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bushin Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Okutsu Shrine ({{ill|lt=Hearth deities|Q249178|qid=Q249178|lt=Okutsuhiko-no-Kami and Okutsuhime-no-Kami|1=Kitchen God}}) * Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kuninotokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|qid=Q478504|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}) * Kuroo Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuroo Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Guardian deity * {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|qid=Q337624|1=Acala}} – Former Buddhist image from the shrine temple === Festivals and annual events === * Hatsu-Ebisu (January {{ill|lt=January 9|Q2238|qid=Q2238|lt=9|1=January 9}}–{{ill|lt=January 11|Q2247|qid=Q2247|lt=11|1=January 11}}) * Yakuyoke Festival (Weekend closest to {{ill|lt=January 18|Q2265|qid=Q2265|1=January 18}}–{{ill|lt=January 19|Q2266|qid=Q2266|lt=19|1=January 19}}) * Kinensai ({{ill|lt=April 23|Q2527|qid=Q2527|1=April 23}}) * Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|qid=Q2657|1=June 30}}) * Annual Festival ({{ill|lt=Health and Sports Day|UNKNOWN|lt=Second Monday of October}}) * {{ill|lt=Niinamesai|Q11501518|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|qid=Q3021|1=November 23}}) === Cultural properties === === Natural monument (designated by Kobe City) === * Shrine grove ({{ill|lt=chinju no mori|Q11651158|qid=Q11651158|1=Chinju no Mori}}; approx. 7,000 m²) – Grove of {{ill|lt=Castanopsis|Q1065400|qid=Q1065400|lt=shii|1=Castanopsis}} trees; designated an environmental green conservation area by Hyōgo Prefecture. === Access === * 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kobe Electric Railway Sanda Line|Q6800409|qid=Q6800409|1=Shintetsu Sanda Line}} {{ill|lt=Okaba Station|Q7081708|qid=Q7081708|1=Okaba Station}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * [{{NDLDC|1174068}} Arima District Council, Arima District Gazette, Vol. 1 – Modern Digital Library] * [{{NDLDC|1174089}} Arima District Council, Arima District Gazette, Vol. 2 – Modern Digital Library] * *History of Arino Village*, Arino Village, 1948 === See also === * {{ill|lt=Yusen Shrine|Q11563671|qid=Q11563671|1=Yusen Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kōchi Shrine|Q11392062|qid=Q11392062|1=Kuchi Shrine}} === External links === * [http://arimajinja.com/ Official website of Arima Shrine] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301114.html Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association – Arima Shrine] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301122.html Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association – Tao Shrine] * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301121.html Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association – Okaba Wakamiya Shrine] {{shinto-stub}}{{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Arima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Arima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:神戸市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kita-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:有野町]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
